summary: Iteration 28 testing complete. All bug fixes verified: (1) Double-tap prevention working - useRef guard prevents duplicate orders on rapid clicks, (2) Order creation with table_id stores and returns table_name, (3) KDS public endpoint enriches orders with table_name from tables collection, (4) Atomic order counter ensures sequential unique order numbers even under concurrent load, (5) POS screen renders correctly at 1920x800 with 6 product columns and 400px cart sidebar, (6) Responsive layout verified: 2 cols mobile, 3 cols tablet, 6 cols desktop.

issues: Console warning: Chart width/height -1 - recharts container sizing issue (non-blocking, visual only)
Console warning: IndexedDB getAll error for offline sync - non-blocking

critical_code_review_comments: Double-tap prevention: POSScreen.js uses useRef (placingOrderRef) for synchronous guard - correctly prevents race conditions that useState would have
Atomic order counter: orders.py uses MongoDB find_one_and_update with $inc on order_counters collection - guarantees unique sequential numbers
Table name storage: Order creation fetches table name from tables collection and stores in order document
KDS enrichment: Both authenticated and public KDS endpoints enrich orders with table_name from tables collection
Printer check: Backend /api/printer/check endpoint provides TCP reachability check as browser fallback
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 28 verified all bug fixes: (1) Double-tap prevention uses useRef for synchronous guard, (2) Orders store table_name when table_id provided, (3) KDS endpoints enrich table info, (4) Atomic counter prevents duplicate order numbers, (5) POS layout correct at 1920x800, (6) Responsive grid: 2/3/6 columns. Printer WiFi detection moved to native TCP - can't test from browser but backend fallback works.

bug_1_double_tap_prevention.evidence: Rapid triple-click on Place Order button created only 1 order (pending count 51→52). Button became disabled after first click.
bug_1_double_tap_prevention.code_review: POSScreen.js line 79: placingOrderRef = useRef(false), line 557-558: synchronous guard check

bug_2_table_name_storage.evidence: API test: POST /api/orders with table_id returns order with table_name field populated

bug_3_kds_table_enrichment.evidence: KDS public endpoint returns orders with table_name enriched from tables collection
bug_3_kds_table_enrichment.api_test: GET /api/kds/public/orders/{restaurant_id}/{token} returns table_name for orders with table_id

bug_4_atomic_order_counter.evidence: Concurrent order creation test: 5 simultaneous orders created with unique sequential numbers
bug_4_atomic_order_counter.code_review: orders.py line 11-22: get_next_order_number uses find_one_and_update with $inc

bug_5_pos_layout_1920x800.evidence: POS at 1920x800: 6 product columns (232px each), cart sidebar 400px, all products visible

bug_6_responsive_layout.evidence: Mobile: 2 cols, Tablet: 3 cols, Desktop: 6 cols. Cart sidebar: hidden on mobile, 340px tablet, 400px desktop

kds_template_styling.evidence: kds_monitor.html: .item-qty 38px/20px (44px/24px on 1920px+), .table-badge blue bg with table_name display

features_verified.order_creation_api: VERIFIED - Creates orders with atomic counter
features_verified.kds_orders_api: VERIFIED - Returns orders with table info
features_verified.kds_stats_api: VERIFIED - Returns queue depth and avg prep time
features_verified.printer_check_api: VERIFIED - Backend TCP check endpoint exists
features_verified.pos_responsive: VERIFIED - Correct columns at all breakpoints

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are real. Printer WiFi detection uses native TCP on APK (untestable in browser), backend fallback tested.

notes_on_untestable_features.printer_wifi_native: WiFi printer detection from tablet uses Capacitor TCP socket plugin - requires native APK, cannot test from browser. Backend /api/printer/check provides fallback for preview testing.
